metadata_source: local apt-cache binary and source package metadata, installed man pages, installed package file lists, installed headers, installed binary/library strings, and local runtime smoke checks from an Ubuntu 24.04 system configured against the Ubuntu 24.04 repositories

selection_scope.goal: Curate a diverse, non-exhaustive set of Ubuntu 24.04 repository packages whose build or runtime behavior depends on liblzma.
selection_policy: Prefer packages with a direct runtime dependency on liblzma5 and a direct source-package Build-Depends on liblzma-dev when both are easy to verify.
Allow indirect runtime paths when the user-facing package clearly routes liblzma-backed behavior through a dependent library package, as with apt and python3.12.

Prefer packages from different domains: package management, language runtimes, XML and image libraries, archive handling, filesystem tooling, kernel module management, debugging, databases, and C++ runtime libraries.
Describe the runtime behavior that actually exercises liblzma instead of only restating that the package depends on it.
notes: This file is intentionally representative rather than exhaustive.
Every listed package has concrete runtime functionality that relies on liblzma; compile-time dependence is included when it was directly verified from Ubuntu source metadata.

dependents.runtime_functionality: Handles xz-compressed members inside .deb archives during package extraction and installation, including archives that use xz for control.tar and data.tar members.
evidence: Ubuntu package metadata: dpkg Pre-Depends on liblzma5.
Ubuntu source metadata: dpkg Build-Depends on liblzma-dev.
The installed deb(5) man page says .deb members may be compressed with xz and names xz support for control.tar and data.tar.
The installed dpkg-deb binary links liblzma and its strings mention xz as an allowed compression type.

dependents.runtime_functionality: Downloads and decompresses xz or lzma-compressed repository metadata and also handles Debian package members such as control.tar.xz, data.tar.xz, and data.tar.lzma through libapt-pkg.
evidence: Ubuntu package metadata: apt depends on libapt-pkg6.0t64, and libapt-pkg6.0t64 depends on liblzma5.
Ubuntu source metadata: apt Build-Depends on liblzma-dev.
The shipped file /usr/share/doc/apt/examples/configure-index declares Acquire::CompressionTypes entries for lzma and xz.
The installed libapt-pkg library links liblzma and its strings mention Acquire::CompressionTypes::xz, Acquire::CompressionTypes::lzma, control.tar.xz, data.tar.xz, and data.tar.lzma.

dependents.runtime_functionality: Provides Python's lzma module so Python code can read and write .xz and raw .lzma streams through APIs such as lzma.open(), LZMAFile, compress(), and decompress().
evidence: Ubuntu package metadata: python3.12 depends on libpython3.12-stdlib, and libpython3.12-stdlib depends on liblzma5.
Ubuntu source metadata: python3.12 Build-Depends on liblzma-dev.
The installed libpython3.12-stdlib package ships /usr/lib/python3.12/lzma.py and /usr/lib/python3.12/lib-dynload/_lzma.cpython-312-x86_64-linux-gnu.so.
A local runtime check with python3.12 successfully imports the lzma module.

dependents.runtime_functionality: Lets libxml2 callers transparently read xz-compressed XML input through the library's I/O layer.
evidence: Ubuntu package metadata: libxml2 depends on liblzma5.
Ubuntu source metadata: libxml2 Build-Depends-Arch on liblzma-dev.
The installed libxml2 README documents the LIBXML2_WITH_LZMA build option and names liblzma as a supported compression library.
The installed libxml2 shared library links liblzma and exposes xz-related symbols such as __libxml2_xzopen, __libxml2_xzread, and __libxml2_xzcompressed.

dependents.runtime_functionality: Reads and writes TIFF images that use the LZMA compression scheme.
evidence: Ubuntu package metadata: libtiff6 depends on liblzma5.
Ubuntu source metadata: tiff Build-Depends on liblzma-dev.
The installed libtiff shared library contains LZMA-specific symbols and strings including TIFFInitLZMA, tif_lzma.c, COMPRESSION_LZMA, and LZMA2 Compression Preset.

dependents.runtime_functionality: The mksquashfs and unsquashfs tools can create and extract SquashFS images that use xz or lzma compression.
evidence: Ubuntu package metadata: squashfs-tools depends on liblzma5.
Ubuntu source metadata: squashfs-tools Build-Depends on liblzma-dev.
The installed mksquashfs and unsquashfs binaries link liblzma.
The installed mksquashfs man page lists xz and lzma among the supported compressors and documents xz and lzma compressor options.

dependents.runtime_functionality: Loads and inspects compressed kernel modules, including .ko.xz modules, through libkmod's xz decompression path.
evidence: Ubuntu package metadata: kmod depends on liblzma5.
Ubuntu source metadata: kmod Build-Depends on liblzma-dev.
The installed kmod binary strings include .ko.xz, load_xz, xz_uncompress_belch, and multiple xz-specific error messages.

dependents.runtime_functionality: Uses liblzma when consuming LZMA-compressed .gnu_debugdata mini-debuginfo sections embedded in ELF binaries.
evidence: Ubuntu package metadata: gdb depends on liblzma5.
Ubuntu source metadata: gdb Build-Depends on liblzma-dev.
The installed gdb binary links liblzma.
The installed gdb binary contains .gnu_debugdata strings including '.gnu_debugdata for %s' and 'Cannot parse .gnu_debugdata section; not a BFD object'.

dependents.runtime_functionality: Reads and writes xz and lzma-compressed archive streams for libarchive-based tools and applications.
evidence: Ubuntu package metadata: libarchive13t64 depends on liblzma5.
Ubuntu source metadata: libarchive Build-Depends on liblzma-dev.
The Ubuntu package description explicitly says the library supports xz and lzma filters using liblzma.
The installed archive.h header declares archive_read_support_filter_xz(), archive_read_support_filter_lzma(), archive_write_add_filter_xz(), and archive_write_add_filter_lzma().

dependents.runtime_functionality: Provides bsdtar and bsdcat so users can create, list, extract, and decompress xz/lzma-backed archive streams through libarchive.
evidence: Ubuntu package metadata: libarchive-tools depends on libarchive13t64, and libarchive13t64 depends on liblzma5.
Ubuntu source metadata: libarchive Build-Depends on liblzma-dev.
A local runtime check with bsdtar successfully created, listed, and extracted a tar.xz archive.
A local runtime check with bsdcat successfully decompressed an xz payload.

dependents.runtime_functionality: Adds LZMA compression support for MariaDB server storage-engine paths, with the package description calling out InnoDB and Mroonga specifically.
evidence: Ubuntu package metadata: mariadb-plugin-provider-lzma depends on liblzma5.
Ubuntu source metadata: mariadb Build-Depends on liblzma-dev.
The Ubuntu package description explicitly says the plugin provides LZMA compression support in the server and storage engines and notes that it affects InnoDB and Mroonga.

dependents.runtime_functionality: Exposes Boost.Iostreams LZMA compressor and decompressor filters to C++ programs that build pipelines around Boost stream abstractions.
evidence: Ubuntu package metadata: libboost-iostreams1.83.0 depends on liblzma5.
Ubuntu source metadata: boost1.83 Build-Depends on liblzma-dev.
The installed header /usr/include/boost/iostreams/filter/lzma.hpp defines lzma_compressor and lzma_decompressor filter types.
